name: QuestDB
description: High-performance open-source time-series database with SQL support, optimized for fast ingestion and low-latency queries

license.comment: Core database is Apache-2.0; Enterprise features require commercial license

hosting-options.comment: QuestDB Cloud was discontinued; now offers Enterprise BYOC (Bring Your Own Cloud) model. Available on AWS Marketplace.

initial-release.comment: Started as side project in 2014 by Vlad Ilyushchenko; incorporated in 2019; YC S20

primary-language.comment: Zero-GC Java and C++ core; Enterprise includes Rust components

data-model.comment: Relational model with column-based storage optimized for time-series workloads

topology.comment: Primary-replica replication (Enterprise); multi-primary ingestion available in Enterprise with FoundationDB-backed sequencer

sharding.comment: Time-based partitioning (hourly, daily, monthly, yearly); no automatic horizontal sharding across nodes in open-source

replication-factor.comment: Replication via object store (S3, Azure Blob, NFS); any number of replicas can read from object store

multi-region.comment: Replication through object store enables geo-distribution; replicas can be in different regions

cap-position.comment: Eventual consistency for replication, but primary provides strong consistency via WAL

consistency-model.comment: Read-committed isolation on primary; eventual consistency for replicas

transaction-scope.comment: Single-writer model with WAL; transactions are table-scoped

distributed-transactions.comment: Multi-primary uses optimistic locking for conflict resolution, not distributed transactions

query-language.comment: Standard SQL with time-series extensions (SAMPLE BY, LATEST ON, ASOF JOIN)

sql-compatibility.comment: PostgreSQL wire protocol compatible for querying; SQL with time-series extensions but missing some standard SQL features

secondary-indexes.comment: Only Symbol column type supports indexing; no general secondary indexes

joins.comment: Supports INNER, LEFT OUTER, CROSS, ASOF, LT, SPLICE, and WINDOW JOINs; hash join and nested loop implementations

aggregations.comment: Full aggregation support including GROUP BY, SAMPLE BY for time bucketing, window functions

full-text-search.comment: String functions and regex matching available, but no dedicated full-text search engine

max-cluster-size.comment: No documented maximum cluster size; horizontal scaling for reads via replicas

write-throughput.comment: 4M rows/sec at low cardinality scaling to 11.4M rows/sec at 100K hosts in TSBS benchmarks; 6-13x faster than TimescaleDB

read-latency-p99.comment: Sub-2ms for single-groupby queries; 40-58ms for full-table double-groupby aggregations

auto-scaling.comment: Manual replica management; no automatic scale-out in open-source

hot-spot-handling.comment: Time-based partitioning; partition strategy must be chosen upfront and cannot be changed

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: With replication, replicas can serve reads during primary upgrade; failover handled automatically in Enterprise

online-schema-changes.comment: Partitioning strategy cannot be changed; column modifications require copy-and-rename pattern

point-in-time-recovery.comment: Via WAL replay from object store; requires replication.recovery.timestamp setting

backup-strategy.comment: CHECKPOINT mode for filesystem snapshots; continuous WAL shipping to object store for replication

kubernetes-operator.comment: Official Helm charts and Kubernetes Operator available

rust-driver.comment: Official questdb-rs crate for ILP ingestion

go-driver.comment: Official Go client for ILP ingestion; PostgreSQL drivers (pgx) for querying

java-driver.comment: Official Java client for ILP; PostgreSQL JDBC driver for querying

python-driver.comment: Official py-questdb-client for ILP; psycopg3 for querying via PostgreSQL wire protocol

ruby-driver.comment: Via PostgreSQL wire protocol compatibility using standard Ruby PostgreSQL gems

c-cpp-driver.comment: Official C/C++ client library built from the Rust client

orm-support.comment: questdb-connect provides SQLAlchemy dialect and Apache Superset integration

open-source.comment: Apache-2.0 licensed; Enterprise features (HA, multi-primary, RBAC) are commercial

free-tier.comment: QuestDB Cloud was discontinued; Enterprise is contact-for-pricing BYOC model

pricing-model.comment: Enterprise pricing is custom/contact-based; open-source is free

cost-estimate-small.comment: No public pricing; Enterprise BYOC requires custom quote

change-data-capture.comment: CDC as a source is an open feature request; QuestDB can receive CDC data but not emit it natively

event-streaming.comment: Official QuestDB Kafka connector (sink) for ingestion from Kafka; integrates with Debezium for CDC pipelines

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Unable to retrieve exact count; questdb tag exists on Stack Overflow
